However, I did find some information about the plant Polycarena linearifolia (not Polycarena linearifoha). Polycarena linearifolia is a small, shrub-like plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to southern Africa. The plant is reported to have several health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, due to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. In traditional medicine, Polycarena linearifolia is often used to treat various ailments, and it is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion.
taxonomy: Polycarena linearifoha belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae, which is an ancient and diverse family of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the figwort family and includes species found in a wide range of habitats. Polycarena linearifoha is a genus of annual or biennial herbs native to southern Africa. It is part of the tribe Limoselleae, which comprises plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Polycarena linearifoha is still being researched and refined by botanists.
